Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

Get details of a specific issue.\n2. get_comments - Get issue comments.\n3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.\n4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.\n","enum":["get","get_comments","get_sub_issues","get_labels"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"The owner of the repository","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"The name of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"issue_read"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List available issue types"},"description":"List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"The organization owner of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["owner"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issue_types"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List issues"},"description":"List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"after":{"description":"Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. 2026-04-07T20:42:10.9997818Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:40:27.860486005Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57884,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document FoundryEnrichmentProcessor span attributes and override behavior\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57877) by `@needuv`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\n`FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` was updated so that `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` are now stamped in `OnEnd` rather than `OnStart`. This ensures those tags take precedence over any values set by underlying frameworks (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) during the span\\u0026#39;s lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be set in `OnStart`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Telemetry** section of the README mentions OpenTelemetry integration but does not describe `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` or the specific span attributes it sets.\\n- The README does not document the environment variables that drive agent identity enrichment (`FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID`) in the context of telemetry.\\n- The README does not explain the `gen_ai.agent.id` derivation (`agentName:agentVersion` when both are present, otherwise `agentName`).\\n- The README does not explain the `OnEnd` override rationale — a key behavioral characteristic for users debugging unexpected span attribute values.\\n- The CHANGELOG for the upcoming `1.0.0-beta.21` release does not mention this behavioral change (tag timing shifted from `OnStart` to `OnEnd`).\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Update CHANGELOG\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — add a new entry in the `1.0.0-beta.21 (Unreleased)` `### Features Added` block.\\n\\nAdd after the existing `FoundryEnvironment` bullet:\\n\\n```markdown\\n- `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` now stamps `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` in `OnEnd` instead of `OnStart`, ensuring they take precedence over any values set by underlying frameworks (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) during a span\\u0026#39;s lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be set in `OnStart`.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Update README Telemetry section\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — expand the existing `### Telemetry` subsection under `## Key concepts` to add the following content after the existing paragraph:\\n\\n```markdown\\n#### Foundry agent span enrichment\\n\\n`FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` is an OpenTelemetry span processor that automatically enriches every span with Foundry agent identity attributes. It is registered automatically when you call `AddAgentHostTelemetry()` — no additional configuration is needed.\\n\\nThe following attributes are set on **every** span:\\n\\n| Attribute | Source environment variable | Set in |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `microsoft.foundry.project.id` | `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID` | `OnStart` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.name` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME` | `OnEnd` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.version` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION` | `OnEnd` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.id` | Derived: `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}:{FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION}` (or `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}` when version is absent) | `OnEnd` |\\n\\n`gen_ai.agent.*` attributes are set in `OnEnd` so that they override any values that an underlying framework (for example the OpenAI SDK) may have stamped during the span\\u0026#39;s lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` is set in `OnStart` because project membership is always known before any work begins.\\n\\nOnly attributes whose corresponding environment variable is set are stamped; unset variables result in the attribute being omitted from the span.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently contains all required sections in the correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Start a server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts (AgentHost, AgentHostBuilder, AgentHostApp, FoundryEnvironment, Telemetry, Health endpoint)\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ✅ Troubleshooting\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\nNo structural changes are needed; only content additions to the **Telemetry** subsection.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no unexpected README changes\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public API changes expected for this processor (it is `internal sealed`), but run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-07T20:42:11.0166916Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:40:51.874767044Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":23,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute
